headline: Multimodal and agentic techniques are breaking out sharply as LLM and benchmark discourse leads the day's rotation.
body: LLM and benchmark coverage is surging as leading movers, coinciding with reports of an imminent GPT-5.6 release and Anthropic's potential trillion-dollar IPO valuation [1][2]. Eight new techniques entered as breakouts simultaneously, with tool calling, task decomposition, reward modeling, and MLLM-as-a-Judge all appearing as new entrants alongside 3D understanding, inverse graphics, code generation, and perceptual perturbation, pointing to a broad expansion in multimodal and agentic evaluation work. On the infrastructure side, NVIDIA's Cosmos 3 and Nemotron 3 Ultra dropped alongside RTX Spark [5], and Mellum2 went open source with a focus on inference acceleration [0]. Colorado rolled back its landmark AI governance law [7], a notable policy reversal as AI risk continues as a leading opinion mover.

cited_items.title: Mellum2 Goes Open Source: A Fast Model for AI Workflows
Anthropic is about to IPO at a TRILLION DOLLARS
[AINews] NVIDIA Cosmos 3, Nemotron 3 Ultra, and RTX Spark
Colorado Rolls Back Landmark AI Governance Law
